title: denhamia fournieri subsp. drakeana
intro: Denhamia fournieri subsp. drakeana, commonly known as the Polynesian tree or Polynesian dogwood, is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands, specifically in New Caledonia and the Loyalty Islands. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its bark and roots, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues like bronchitis. In traditional medicine, Denhamia fournieri subsp. drakeana is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in the form of topical creams or ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
